He soon finds himself helping other patients as a serial killer begins killing them one by one.

programme.title: My Baby Gone

programme.title: Before I Go To Sleep [Broadcast Edit]
programme.desc: Ever since a vicious attack nearly claimed her life, Christine Lucas (Nicole Kidman) has suffered from anterograde amnesia and is unable to form new memories. Every morning, she becomes reacquainted with her husband, Ben (Colin Firth), and the other constants in her life. In accordance with her doctor's (Mark Strong) instructions, Christine keeps a video diary. As Christine starts to uncover terrifying truths about her past, she begins to question everything -- and everyone -- around her.

programme.title: Eat Pray Love

programme.title: Girl, Interrupted
programme.desc: Set in the changing world of the late 1960 s, GIRL INTERRUPTED is the searing story of Susanna Kaysen (Ryder), a young woman who finds herself at a renowned mental institution for troubled women. Based on the best-selling novel of the same name and directed by celebrated director James Mangold, GIRL INTERRUPTED features tour de force performance by todays most critically acclaimed young actors. Kaysen s prescribed "short rest" from a psychiatrist she had met only once becomes a strange, unknown journey into Alice s Wonderland, where she struggles with the thin line between normal and crazy. Kaysen soon realizes how hard it is to get out once she has been comitted, and she ultimatley has to choose between the world of people who belong inside or the difficult world of reality outside.

programme.title: The Lazarus Project
programme.desc: A harrowing and frightening thriller about a man who has everything he's ever loved stripped away from him; and to earn his life and family back, he must face obstacles of mystical origins, endure countless tests of his faith, struggle with his own sanity, and explore the depth and the power of his soul.

programme.title: The Caine Mutiny
programme.desc: A naval lieutenant is court-martialed for seizing command of a minesweeper from a neurotic commander (Humphrey Bogart).

programme.title: It Had To Be You
programme.desc: Ginger Rogers stars in this fanciful, screwball comedy as a young woman who has left three grooms standing at the altar...and is about to do it again! Cornel Wilde co-stars as the gentleman who tempts her away from the altar and then succeeds in marrying her himself.

programme.title: The Trouble With Angels
programme.desc: A pair of young troublemakers is assigned to a convent school. Try as she may, the Reverend Mother is unable to stop the two from wreaking havoc on the place.

programme.title: The Whole Town's Talking
programme.desc: A mild-mannered clerk's life is turned upside down by his exact double -- a ruthless gangster and Public Enemy Number One.

programme.title: Elegy
programme.desc: David Kepesh is growing old. He's a professor of literature, a student of American hedonism, and an amateur musician and photographer. When he finds a student attractive, Consuela, a 24-year-old Cuban, he sets out to seduce her. Along the way, he swims in deeper feelings, maybe he's drowning. She presses him to sort out what he wants from her, and a relationship develops. They talk of traveling. He confides in his friend, George, a poet long-married, who advises David to grow up and grow old. She invites him to meet her family. His own son, from a long-ended marriage, confronts him. Is the elegy for lost relationships, lost possibilities, beauty and time passing, or failure of nerve?

programme.title: Serving Sara
programme.desc: Joe Tyler is a process server who will do anything to deliver legal documents to victims. But nothing in Joe's bag of tricks prepares him for Sara, the stunning, soon-to-be ex-wife of a playboy cattle baron named Gordon Moore.

programme.title: Shaolin Wooden Men
programme.desc: After witnessing the murder of his father, Little Mute trains in Shaolin Temple for revenge and is taught by various teachers, one of which is clandestine.

programme.title: Undisputed 4: Boyka

programme.title: Dead Ringers
programme.desc: Twin gynecologists take full advantage of the fact that nobody can tell them apart, until their relationship begins to deteriorate over a woman.

programme.desc: A detective gets kicked off the force when the murder of his wife drives him to drink. When a new string of murders seem connected to her death, he soon finds himself squaring off against a killer who appears to be superhuman.

programme.title: A Witness to Murder

programme.title: New Dogs, Old Tricks

programme.title: New Dogs, Old Tricks

programme.title: Call Her King
programme.desc: After sentencing a notorious gangster to death, Judge Jaeda King must rely on her wits, strength and skill when her courtroom is taken over by force by the prisoner's brother.

programme.title: CODE
programme.desc: Dallas artist MALIK SCHAEFER, also known as CODE, is a rising star in the underground rap scene. Although his checkered past has led him down the wrong road at times, with the help of his best friend, STYLES, Malik is back on the fast-track to success. That is, until he reconnects with the dangerous love of his life, SOPHIA ORTEGA, who threatens to destroy it all. Malik must decide to either follow his heart or live by the code that has served him well for many years: family over everything.

programme.title: The New Girl

She then launches her own investigation and unearths a twisted web of lies, abuse, and even political conspiracy. Malorie must confront dangerous secrets that not only threaten her sister's life but her own.

programme.title: Threaded Lies
programme.desc: When rising fashion designer Dante prepares to launch his brand, haunting messages and resurfaced betrayals from his former partner threaten to unravel everything. As paranoia sets in, Dante must confront the lies he’s built his success on before they destroy him.

Now in a coma, Kate's only hope is Detective Sean McKinney, who desperately tries to clear her name. But unbeknownst to him, Maniac Cop Matt Cordell takes it upon himself to exact revenge.

programme.title: Creepshow
programme.desc: Jack's mother is taken from him, but her garden leaves more to remember her by than he first thought. Then, three teens meet their favorite pop star and realize she's not at all what they imagined.

programme.title: Creepshow

programme.title: Creepshow

programme.title: Creepshow
programme.desc: Cassie is being chased by a monster, and endangers a kind stranger when she asks for help- but what can they do? Then, a celebrated photographer is tormented after an award-winning photograph has more consequences than just fame.

programme.title: Creepshow
programme.desc: A horror novelist's agent offers him help overcoming his writer's block, and the struggling author can't resist. Then, Richard's wife is attacked and becomes a shell of the woman he loved. He'll do anything to help her feel better.

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies
programme.desc: "Pygmalion and Elly" - Sonny Drysdale decides he needs to be Pygmalion to Elly's Galatea and remake her from a hillbilly into a woman of society, while Granny uses love charms to heat up their relationship.

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies
programme.desc: "Elly's First Date" - Mr. Drysdale convinces his self-absorbed stepson Sonny to date Elly May to Mrs. Drysdale's consternation, while Granny and Jed make preparations for Thanksgiving.

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies
programme.desc: "Jethro Goes to School" - Jed enrolls Jethro at an exclusive Beverly Hills elementary school. Millicent Schuyler-Potts, the proprietor of the school, is aghast when Jed and Jethro show up, convinced this is part of some ghastly hoax. After Jed says that banker Milburn Drysdale is his neighbor, she calls the banker. It turns out that Drysdale's bank holds the mortgage on the school. Schulyer-Potts suddenly warms to the charms of the Clampetts, even if it means accepting Jethro as a fifth grader.

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies
programme.desc: "The Servants" - Ellie May starts wearing dresses but takes it as an insult when someone says she looks like a city girl. Mr. Drysdale talks Jed into taking in his butler and upstairs girl in a plan to civilize the Clampetts, but they don't quite understand the idea of servants. Miss Hathaway takes Jethro bowling and begins to show an interest in him.

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies
programme.desc: "Trick or Treat" - Granny wants to go home because "folks is so unfriendly, no one has come to call." Jed convinces her that they should call on them, and they find that people welcome them and give them all manner of gifts when they show up on Halloween in their normal hillbilly clothes. Also, Jed works on getting Pearl to come visit while she's trying to get hitched to the oilman and find a young man for Jethrine.

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies
programme.desc: "The Race for Queen" - Elly May competes in a contest to determine "The Queen of Beverly Hills."

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies
programme.desc: "Lafe Lingers On" - Lafe Crick comes back in another scheme to get rich off the Clampetts' wealth.

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies
programme.desc: "The Girl From Home" - A father and daughter from back home come to Beverly Hills to meet Jed.

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies
programme.desc: Mr. Drysdale's idea for Christmas presents are ones only fit for coastal California, like diving suits and a boat, and a television set, which Granny thinks is some kind of fancy washing machine.

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies
programme.desc: "The Clampetts Get Culture" - The Clampetts decide to get a taste of culture to fit in the high-class society of Beverly Hills.

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies
programme.desc: "The Garden Party" - Mrs. Drysdale has a garden party and 'invites' the Clampetts by mistake. They end up back by their 'cement pond' to handle the 'overflow' from Mrs. Drysdale's party. A gun shot by Granny starts the real party.

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies
programme.desc: "Turkey Day" - Elly bonds with the Thanksgiving turkey so the family can't bring themselves to kill it; Mrs Drysdale hires some Indians to take a Thanksgiving picture, which puts Granny on the warpath.

programme.desc: "The Clampetts Go Hollywood" - When Jake Clampett tells Jed he's his cousin, he's welcomed into the home and soon has everyone but Jed thinking about and dressing for a role in their next movie.

programme.desc: Lucas and Mark find themselves exposed to anthrax after they adopt a stray horse whose owner has been killed in a gunfight.

programme.title: The Pathfinder

With the help of Carter and Silverheels, he learns that the French are planning to take control of the Great Lakes. The French unmask Montgomery and Carter, and British officer Lester and his troops save the couple from the firing squad. Based on James Fenimore Cooper's novel.

programme.title: Man In The Saddle
programme.desc: A small-time rancher loses his girl to a big-time rancher. She sees the error of her ways only too late.

programme.title: The Deadly Companions
programme.desc: An unholy alliance! Marking the feature film debut of one of Hollywoods most influential and widely referenced auteur directors, Sam Peckinpah, The Deadly Companions centers on a redemptive journey to escort a funeral procession through dangerous Indian Territory. Featuring fine work from a top-notch cast that includes the inimitable Maureen OHara at the height of her illustrious career, Peckinpahs first foray into film provides a promising glance into the energetic and violent nature of his iconic later works.

programme.title: West of the Divide
programme.desc: John Wayne stars as Ted Hayden, a tough hombre who goes undercover to join a gang led by a heartless fiend known only as Mr. Gentry (Lloyd Whitlock). Gentry is out to take over a ranch belonging to one of Hayden's friends, but as Hayden finds out, Gentry has a lot more than that up his sleeve. You'll have to watch this two-fisted Western to find out the rest!

programme.title: Randy Rides Alone
programme.desc: John Wayne was 27 when he starred in this vintage western drama from Hollywoods Poverty Row. He plays Randy Bowers, a man wrongly jailed for murders. With help from a female friend, he breaks out of prison and sets out to find the real killers. Harry L. Fraser (The Last of the Clintons, Rustlers Paradise) directs a strong cast of familiar faces, including legendary sidekick George Gabby Hayes, Earl Dwyer, and one of the hardest ridin stuntmen turned actors, Yakima Canutt.

programme.title: The Range Busters
programme.desc: In the first of the Range Buster series, a mysterious figure is killing the help at the Circle T Ranch. The Range Busters are called in to help, but cant figure out the murderers identity. Is it Torrence, a longtime rival of the Circle T, or is it blind uncle Rolph? Ray Crash Corrigan, John Dusty King, and Alibi Max Terhune head the cast. Director S. Roy Luby (Border Phantom, Black Market Rustlers), also has a fine list of supporting players, including Luana Walters, Leroy Mason, and Kermit Maynard.

programme.title: The Fighting Redhead
programme.desc: When a rustler named Faro kills one of his own henchmen and blames it on Sheila O'Conner, Red and Little Beaver set out to clear Sheila and to find evidence against Faro and his gang.

programme.title: Red Ryder: Ride, Ryder, Ride!
programme.desc: Red Ryder encounters bandits trying to hold up the stagecoach carrying the owner of the Devil's Hole newspaper, Libby Brooks and her brother Gerry. Red successfully drives off the attackers but becomes embroiled in further conflict, leading to a duel that nearly claims his life.

programme.title: Springtime in the Sierras
programme.desc: Ads for this Roy Rogers classic roared HEAD FOR THE MUSICAL HILLS...ROY IS GUNNIN' FOR THRILLS! And thrills are what you'll get when you watch this fun, action packed B-Western from Republic Pictures. There's some great comic relief from Andy Devine and Chester Conklin, two of Hollywood's all-time greats. This old-fashioned shoot-'em-up was directed by William Witney, who would go on to direct many of television's top western sagas, including Bonanza, Wagon Train, and The Wild, Wild West.

programme.title: Song of Arizona

One of the boys uses stolen money to help Gabby pay off his debts, but things unravel when the boys father, a notorious outlaw named King Blaine, is murdered; the rest of the Blaine gang show up at Gabbys looking for the loot! Director Frank McDonald works with a fine cast, including Dale Evans, Lyle Talbot, and of course, Roys beloved horse Trigger.

programme.title: Strongman Champions League

The fifth event, a front hold of 25 kg is loved or hated by the athletes and lastly, the Stones of Strength.

programme.title: Strongman Champions League

programme.title: Chewed Up
programme.desc: Clinton Kelly takes the lead in the kitchen as he prepares a nostalgic Quiche Lorraine inspired by his mother, making the case for a simple yet meaningful Mother’s Day brunch made at home. As he walks through the process—layering raw onions, crispy bacon, and a rich egg-and-cream custard into a par-baked crust—Carla Hall and Michael Symon chime in with their own takes on brunch traditions, ingredient preferences, and the beauty of regional cuisine. The trio shares laughs over everything from vintage baby names to the infamous “Real Men Don’t Eat Quiche” era, using humor to dismantle outdated ideas around food and masculinity. Along the way, they highlight the importance of texture and balance, celebrating the silky, slightly jiggly custard as the hallmark of a perfectly baked quiche. A fan interaction segment adds a personal touch, with a “Fan of the Day” selected for a custom video message, reinforcing the show’s community-driven spirit. Once the quiche comes out of the oven, the hosts dig in, offering real-time reactions and thoughtful observations about flavor, technique, and versatility—debating whether quiche is best suited for brunch, lunch, or dinner. The episode wraps with a heartfelt nod to mothers everywhere and a reminder that sometimes the simplest dishes, made with care, are the most memorable.

programme.title: Simply Symons
programme.desc: Michael Symon, Liz Symon, and Olivia Annacone Cisic tackle a brunch-inspired twist on eggs Benedict by swapping traditional Canadian bacon for crispy pastrami and pairing it with a mustard-spiked hollandaise sauce. Michael walks through a foolproof blender hollandaise method while explaining the science behind emulsifying butter and keeping the sauce warm, making the intimidating brunch staple feel approachable for home cooks. As the family cooks together, the conversation bounces between Olivia’s skepticism about poached eggs, Liz’s love of runny yolks, and memories of iconic pastrami spots like Langer’s and Katz’s. The group also debates poaching techniques, from classic whirlpool methods to muffin tin hacks for feeding a crowd. Humor carries the episode throughout as Michael teases Olivia’s picky eating habits and the family jokes about “buttering muffins” and hollandaise disasters. Liz complements the rich brunch dish with a citrus-forward mimosa made from blood oranges, sumo oranges, lemon juice, and frozen strawberries used as flavorful ice cubes. Once plated, the pastrami Benedict wins Olivia over completely, with the crispy pastrami and tangy mustard hollandaise becoming the standout combination. The episode wraps with the family enjoying brunch cocktails, discussing make-ahead egg tips, and Michael reminding viewers that restaurant-quality brunch can come together quickly and simply at home.

programme.title: Chewed Up
programme.desc: Clinton Kelly wraps up Home Alone Week with the dish his husband Damon absolutely refuses to eat: a comforting Indian-inspired Tofu Sag Paneer riff that Clinton makes happily for himself when he has the kitchen to himself. The hosts celebrate the week's revelation that the most personal food choices are often the ones that define us most.

programme.title: Chewed Up
programme.desc: A special compilation episode looking back at the best Chew It or Screw It moments from the past six months, featuring some of the show's most memorable guests playing along as the hosts ask them to weigh in on food, lifestyle, and totally random preferences. Segments include: Danielle Sepsy (the Scone Queen) forced to choose between scones and muffins—predictably choosing scones—plus her takes on stuffing vs. mashed potatoes and canned vs. homemade cranberry sauce; Liz Symon (Michael's wife) on orange wine, foofy cocktails, lazy sundaes, the New York Times Spelling Bee app (Queen Bee 99% of the time), stinky cheese, and the truth about cuddling with Michael; Marysol Castro on Citi Field hot dogs, pineapple on pizza (a hard Bronx no), and celebrity cameos at sporting events (the exception being Bad Bunny); Amanda Freitag and the crew on going out to eat on Valentine's Day (screw it—you get the worst service and cooks who rarely go out); Devyn Simone on cooking for someone on a first date (surprisingly chew it, as long as boundaries are established) and not texting the next day after getting lucky; Esther Choi on breakfast for dinner, ketchup on eggs (a unanimous screw it from the entire panel); Scott Conant on sending food back at a restaurant (screw it—you're an adult, figure it out), family style with strangers (chew it), and splitting the check 12 ways; Damaris Phillips on cornbread with sugar, Miracle Whip with bologna, and the boat-ride romance that made her appreciate Miracle Whip on a sandwich; Maneet Chauhan on spicy desserts, taking the last bite when sharing a dish (chew it if you're single and looking for a partner), and eating off someone else's plate; Mika Leon on the five-second rule, helicopter parenting, staying up 24 hours to cook (chew it—she did 32-plus hours on a cooking competition), and going to the club (hard pass, she's tired); and Chef Jackie Bula on air fryers (screw it—just fry), and reality cooking competitions (screw it—it's less about the food than the show). Audience engagement prompts woven throughout invite viewers to comment on their own chew-it-or-screw-it takes.

programme.title: Chewed Up
programme.desc: The Chewed Up crew sets up poolside at the W Hotel South Beach for a breezy St. Patrick's Day episode that swaps corned beef for Cuban grilled shrimp. Michael Symon, whose Cleveland neighborhood is deeply Irish, admits he's been celebrating St. Patrick's Day with his best friend Jimmy for decades, and this year Jimmy joins them in Miami. Michael's Grilled Shrimp Mojo is a tribute to the Cuban flavors of Miami: shrimp marinated in lime zest, lime juice, chili flakes, and olive oil, then grilled and finished with a vibrant mojo sauce blended from sour orange, onion, a whole head of garlic, cilantro, parsley, and oregano. The key tip: cook shrimp until they look like commas, not O'sóthe moment they curl completely is the moment they're overdone. Clinton reflects on his seven years living in Miami and the city's culinary depth, from Cuban staples to modern restaurants like Yardbird. Carla raises her fear of sharks; Clinton counters with his inexplicable terror of birds getting caught in his hair. Michael confesses he bought a bathing suit specifically to go to the beach, put it on, saw himself in the mirror, and decided to spend the afternoon in bed instead.

programme.title: Chewed Up
programme.desc: Just in time for National Ravioli Day, acclaimed chef and Chopped judge Scott Conant joins the trio at the W South Beach to make his delicate ricotta gnocchiótechnically gnudióalongside his signature Martone Street jarred tomato sauce. Michael has been close friends with Scott for 15 years, and Clinton admits he was genuinely nervous before meeting the notoriously stern Chopped judge, quickly discovering he's warm and funny in person. Scott explains that Martone Street is named for his grandparents' home in Waterbury, Connecticut, where Sunday dinners taught him that conversation over food is one of life's greatest gifts. The gnocchi dough combines fresh ricotta, basil, breadcrumbs, flour, egg, and Parmesan, shaped into small dumplings and cooked until they float. Scott builds a stabilized butter sauce using pasta water and cornstarch as an emulsifieróa trick that keeps it smooth for hours. Finished with Martone Street sauce and broccoli rabe, the result is stunning in its simplicity. His philosophy runs throughout: spaghetti aglio e olio, done correctly, is one of the most complex and satisfying dishes imaginable. Scott becomes emotional talking about watching his 16-year-old daughter drive for the first time and the joy of steak cook-offs with his girls at home.

programme.title: Chewed Up
programme.desc: Food Network Star winner and Southern food authority Damaris Phillips brings a taste of Louisville poolside at the W South Beach, demonstrating three Southern tea sandwich classics that are anything but fussy. Carla Hall has been singing Damaris's praises for yearsómutual friend Jackie Guido insisted they were destined to be peas and carrotsóand the episode proves her right, the two radiating warmth and joy throughout. Damaris makes Pimento Cheese; Benedictine, a Louisville specialty of grated cucumber with dill and cream cheese (the key being squeezing out all the cucumber water first); and Ham Salad, essentially chicken salad's porky cousin, versatile as either sweet or savory. Everything is layered on Japanese milk bread or thin white bread, crusts removed, and assembled into dainty finger sandwiches and triple-decker stacks. Michael immediately tries to build an enormous open-face sandwich rather than the delicate bites Damaris intended, sparking a lively debate about proportion and restraint. Carla shares that she recently spent a cozy Super Sunday with Damaris in Louisvilleóa beloved tradition of soups, board games, and community. The episode also features a spirited group effort to talk Damaris out of her plan to get a leopard-print face tattoo, with limited success. The episode ends with genuine laughter, warm hugs, and unanimous agreement: the sandwiches are delicious.

programme.title: Chewed Up
programme.desc: Two-time Tournament of Champions winner and Chopped veteran Maneet Chauhan arrives at the W South Beach as a true Queen of Flavor, demonstrating her Indian Spiced Ceviche, a dish that applies Indian culinary logic to coastal Latin technique. Fresh fish is cured in lime juice in the traditional ceviche manner, then transformed with coconut milk to soften the heat and balance the citrus, and finished with a tarka: the Indian technique of blooming whole spices in searing hot coconut oil and pouring it directly over the dish at the last second. The result is layered, bright, and unexpected. Maneet explains that she grew up in a small town in India and learned to cook by exploring her neighbors' kitchens, each representing a different regional tradition. She recounts a pivotal story from Peru, where she made potato pancakes alongside a local woman without sharing a single word of common languageóconnected entirely through food and gesture. That philosophy runs through her Nashville restaurant, which introduces diners to bold Indian flavors through fusion dishes like hot chicken korma and chicken tikka salad poutine. She draws a parallel between the tarka and the French beurre blancóboth finishing techniques that transform a dish in the final seconds of cooking. Michael, Clinton, and Carla taste the ceviche with genuine surprise and awe.

programme.title: Simply Symons
programme.desc: Michael Symon, Liz Symon, and Olivia Annacone Cisic tackle a brunch-inspired twist on eggs Benedict by swapping traditional Canadian bacon for crispy pastrami and pairing it with a mustard-spiked hollandaise sauce. Michael walks through a foolproof blender hollandaise method while explaining the science behind emulsifying butter and keeping the sauce warm, making the intimidating brunch staple feel approachable for home cooks. As the family cooks together, the conversation bounces between Olivia’s skepticism about poached eggs, Liz’s love of runny yolks, and memories of iconic pastrami spots like Langer’s and Katz’s. The group also debates poaching techniques, from classic whirlpool methods to muffin tin hacks for feeding a crowd. Humor carries the episode throughout as Michael teases Olivia’s picky eating habits and the family jokes about “buttering muffins” and hollandaise disasters. Liz complements the rich brunch dish with a citrus-forward mimosa made from blood oranges, sumo oranges, lemon juice, and frozen strawberries used as flavorful ice cubes. Once plated, the pastrami Benedict wins Olivia over completely, with the crispy pastrami and tangy mustard hollandaise becoming the standout combination. The episode wraps with the family enjoying brunch cocktails, discussing make-ahead egg tips, and Michael reminding viewers that restaurant-quality brunch can come together quickly and simply at home.

programme.title: Chewed Up
programme.desc: The South Beach week closes with a live audience tapingóthe most unhinged episode of the tripóas Michael Symon, Carla Hall, and Clinton Kelly perform in front of a crowd of fans. Michael's voice is noticeably shot from a long week of celebrating, so Clinton and Carla carry extra energy while he freestyle hosts from notes he promptly loses within 30 seconds. Carla makes her refreshing Pineapple Lime Agua Fresca: blended fresh pineapple with lime water (lime juice diluted to avoid sharpness), ginger, honey, and wateróa batched mocktail or cocktail mixer that's as beautiful as it is easy. She shares her tip for getting full use of citrus by zesting before juicing. The episode features 'Chew It or Screw It,' a voting game where the audience weighs in on Florida's most adventurous foods: iguana pizza, python sausage, alligator ribs, conch fritters, and fried frog legs. Carla and Clinton report on their spontaneous 7 AM sunrise bike rideótheir first ever togetheróand Clinton jokes about his impossibly pale, non-tanning legs. The trio explores Florida's wildlife peculiarities, from iguanas falling from trees in cold snaps to flying cockroaches given affectionate nicknames. A Chewed Up Challenge heads-up guessing game rounds out the audience interaction with giveaways. Season 3's Miami week ends exactly how it began: loud, funny, and hungry.

programme.title: Chewed Up
programme.desc: Season 3 returns to the studio with a spring episode that puts handmade pasta front and center. Michael Symon makes his grandmother's Cavatellióa ricotta-based pasta dough (1 cup flour, 1 egg, 1.5 cups ricotta, Parmesan) that is more forgiving than traditional egg pasta, closer in spirit to a dumpling than a proper noodle. He demonstrates the technique his grandmother taught him as a child: roll the dough into thin strands, cut into inch-long pieces, and press-roll each one with a butter knife to create a hollow, ridged shape that opens slightly when cooked and catches every drop of sauce. Carla has a revelation mid-episode realizing cavatelli is essentially a dumplingóthe Italian version of a global tradition that spans every culture. The sauce is spring itself: brown butter carried almost to the edge, blanched peas, bias-cut asparagus, pasta water, and lemon. Michael also reflects on the memory of cooking as familyópasta making as a group project, a Sunday activity with kids or grandkids, something imperfect that matters anyway. The episode weaves in conversations about spring weather chaos on the East Coast, Gen X collectively aging past the Golden Girls cast, Michael's habit of texting Cleveland friends his Venice Beach weather reports, and a team-meeting story where Nick's extended hand produced entirely different reactions from all three hosts based on their respective cultural backgrounds. Nick debuts a new segment, 'Choose Wisely,' a spring-edition 'most likely to' game. Sponsored by Fresh Direct and Front Gate.

programme.title: Chewed Up
programme.desc: Top Chef alum and Carla Hall's culinary producer Ariane Duarte joins the Chewed Up crew to make spring crostini three ways on homemade sourdough focaccia, while the hosts debate food rules and play Chew It or Screw It.

programme.title: Chewed Up
programme.desc: Carla Hall makes a vibrant frozen pea soup in under 20 minutes—sautéed onions, stock, mint, parsley, white pepper, and cream blended silky smooth—then leads the hosts in Peep Roulette, a blind flavor-guessing game. Clinton wins; Michael is traumatized.

programme.title: Recipe Club
programme.desc: "Recipe Club: Wonder Bread: A Trashy Version of Eggplant Parm" - An American icon gets a (vaguely) Italian treatment.

programme.title: The Dave Chang Show
programme.desc: Dave cooks a protein-focused dish for a "master of meat": actor, comedian, & host Joel McHale...but Dave doesn't cook red meat like Joel may be used to. After having some of Dave's delicious Wu's Garden-inspired braised tofu, Joel hops (literally) into the kitchen to make a gorgeous Miyazaki A5 wagyu steak. Amongst the cooking, Dave and Joel debate how often you should eat meat, talk about getting older and their health, and reveal why some people call Joel's home "Crazy House" (Hint: Joel likes knives. A lot.).

programme.title: The Dave Chang Show
programme.desc: Dave tries his hand at a Greek dish, avgolemono, and turns it into 3 dishes for a surprise drop-in guest, chef-owner of BIANCO restaurants, Chris Bianco. They talk about pressure cookers, the ultimate Italian soups, and lemon-forward food, amongst other topics. Dave finishes with an idea to take this dish from Greece to another country in a future episode.

programme.title: The Dave Chang Show
programme.desc: Dave makes an incredibly unlikely dish for him to make: a casserole. Dave has Oscar and Emmy-winning writer/director Cord Jefferson as his guest and makes chicken divan to try to bring him back to his childhood. The duo talk their recent trip to Las Vegas together, Cord's unusual route to where he is today, and their philosophies on failure and success. They talk about maintaining a worldly curiosity, trying new things, and how learning what you don't like can be just as important as learning what you do, then finish with a MOIF about Vegas.

programme.title: The Dave Chang Show
programme.desc: Dave makes tteokbokki, a classic and delicious Korean street food, three different ways. He talks about the genius of how the dish is served, how gnocchi may have opened a pathway to tteok acceptance in America, and his love for soft-chewy texture, with hopes it might someday be as beloved by American diners as it is by him. He also answers an Ask Dave about his "broke chef" days.

programme.title: The Dave Chang Show
programme.desc: Dave is joined by MS NOW senior correspondent and author of the book Firestorm, Jacob Soboroff. Dave makes a lettuce cup dish that takes Jacob back to his past, and then they discuss the Los Angeles fires, the subject of Jacob's new book, as well as his personal experiences having grown up in the area. They finish with a MOIF comparing Los Angeles and New York in myriad ways.

programme.title: The Dave Chang Show
programme.desc: Dave is joined by friend of the show and host of Recipe Club, Chris Ying, and producer (Young) David Meyer. Dave serves a Chinese chicken salad and some leftover jangjorim, then the trio dives into some real "Inappropriate Dinner Conversations" in a new game version that reveals a lot about the three contestants.

programme.title: The Dave Chang Show
programme.desc: Dave is joined by a friend of the podcast, Noelle, partner at Majordomo Media, as he cooks her a meaningful dish that she did not know he was going to make. They talk about Paris (related to the dish), changes in the global dining scene, and some necessary improvements on the rules of sharing food.

programme.title: Jacques Pépin Cooking at Home
programme.desc: Jacques celebrates birthdays, anniversaries and holidays with special meals. It’s when he splurges on caviar, oysters, even truffles–the ultimate extravagance. These occasions are a time to gather together around the table with family and friends and indulge in our favorite foods and wines.

programme.title: Pati's Mexican Table
programme.desc: Pati travels to Milpa Alta to harvest and char nopales with Chef Jorge Córcega, then joins Luis Alvarado’s family for their storied mole. Over a shared meal, they celebrate how tradition and flavor nourish the community.

programme.title: Pati's Mexican Table
programme.desc: Pati visits Tlatelolco’s Gris with Gabriela Rojas to reflect on tragedy and hope, then learns danzón at Salón Los Ángeles with Miguel Nieto. With friends, they share chamorro de cerdo and toast resilience with creative micheladas.

programme.title: Pati's Mexican Table
programme.desc: Pati tours Mexico City’s changing skyline on the Iztapalapa Cable Bus, builds a bamboo bike at Bamboocycles, and enjoys tacos and tlacoyos, exploring how design, transit, and creativity shape a sustainable, connected city.

programme.title: Pati's Mexican Table
programme.desc: Pati traces Mexico City’s past with Erika Zúñiga, from Templo Mayor to Bellas Artes and a churro stop at El Moro. With Enrique Acevedo at Central de Abastos, they enjoy tacos de lengua and explore the city’s vibrant present and future.

programme.title: Hawaii Cooks with Roy Yamaguchi - Hilo, Hawaii
programme.desc: Famous for its rainy climate and mild temperatures the Hilo district on the Big Island provides the ideal conditions for growing macadamia nuts. In celebration of the macadamia nut, Hilo is host to an annual macadamia nut festival that brings the local community and guest chefs together.

programme.title: Hawaii Cooks with Roy Yamaguchi - Soy Sauce
programme.desc: Asia’s most popular flavoring is soy sauce. For more than 70 years, the Honolulu Sake Brewery produced both sake and soy sauce. Located at the mouth of Pauoa Valley, the brewery was one of the area's most famous landmarks, until it was torn down in 1993. Hawaii Cooks visits with the former owner of the brewery to reminisce about the history and variety of products they produced.

programme.title: Hawaii Cooks with Roy Yamaguchi - Kauai
programme.desc: Hawaii's delicious bounty of exotic fruit is highlighted with a visit to Kahili Farms in Kilauea, Kauai. Farmer Mike Strong grows rambutan, sugar loaf pineapple, sunrise papaya, caimito, sapodilla and a variety of other exotic fruits. Kahili Farms has successfully recovered from a hurricane and is looking towards exporting exotic fruits nationally and internationally.

programme.title: Hawaii Cooks with Roy Yamaguchi - Kaunakakai, Molokai
programme.desc: Cooking with flavored oils and vinegars is finding increasing popularity. Hawaii Cooks travels to the tiny town of Kaunakakai, Molokai to find out how Maggie Nevin brews Molokai Maggies flavored vinegars. Once a corporate executive, Nevin was looking for a more serene lifestyle. Her answer was to live on Molokai and produce specialty vinegars.

programme.title: Hawaii Cooks with Roy Yamaguchi - Windward, Oahu
programme.desc: First inhabited around 300 AD, most historians consider Windward, Oahu to be the site of the first native Hawaiian settlement. Abundant in freshwater and rich soil, Windward, Oahu is a place where the past converges with the future. Here, farmers still cultivate taro, a staple of the native Hawaiian diet.

programme.title: Hawaii Cooks with Roy Yamaguchi - Maui Pineapple
programme.desc: The Maui Pineapple Company operates the last pineapple canning operation in Hawaii. Hawaii Cooks visits the cannery to find out what makes the Maui Pineapple Company worth maintaining despite its high production costs. In the Kitchen, Roy uses Hawaii's most famous fruit to prepare a pineapple broth served with roasted pork.

programme.title: Hawaii Cooks with Roy Yamaguchi - Waikiki Fieldtrip
programme.desc: Hawaii Cooks explores the nooks and crannies of one the world’s most famous resorts to find the hidden treasures of Waikiki. Some of the “finds” include a rooftop hydroponic garden and a visit to the elegant and historical Halekulani hotel. In the Kitchen, Roy prepares a Hawaiian style stuffed chicken.

programme.title: Hawaii Cooks with Roy Yamaguchi - Chocolate and Vanilla
programme.desc: Chocolate and vanilla may be the two most popular types of ice cream, but they are also two very important specialty crops being grown on the Big Island, the only place in the United States. Hawaii Cooks visits The Original Hawaiian Chocolate Factory and Hawaiian Vanilla Company. Guest chefs, brothers Philippe and Pierre Padovani of Padovani’s Bistro, join Roy in the kitchen.

programme.title: Hawaii Cooks with Roy Yamaguchi - Chinese New Year
programme.desc: Kung Hee Fat Choy! Hawaii Cooks celebrates Chinese New Year with a trip into Chinatown and examines the delicious food and festivities that mark this holiday. Guest chef and Chinese restaurateur, Leeann Chin, joins Roy in the kitchen to prepare a traditional shrimp and tofu dish while discussing the significance of food and this auspicious celebration in Chinese culture.

programme.title: Hawaii Cooks with Roy Yamaguchi - Pasta and Tomatoes
programme.desc: Fresh and simple ingredients combine to make the most satisfying meals. And, what could be more satisfying than fresh pasta and vine-ripened tomatoes. Hawaii Cooks visits C&C Pasta Co., a quaint restaurant and deli, where fresh pasta is the featured item, and Roy checks out Green Growers Farm, a unique hydroponic tomato farm that is churning out fresh-off-the-vine heirloom tomatoes.

programme.desc: The Greeks get their socks knocked off with a spicy Mexican arsenal, but first they have a few things to show our host. Paige learns about the unique culture of Thessaloniki, and at the School of Aristotle, food and history converge.

programme.title: Wild Travels
programme.desc: Visit the fun and lively 100th Anniversary of Green County's Cheese Days festival and tour a Boeing 727 airplane home. Also, experience the World's Largest Rattlesnake Roundup, meet a classic barber pole maker, and relish mustard heaven.

programme.title: Wild Travels
programme.desc: Get hairy at the World Beard and Moustache Championship and taste extraordinary ice cream at Salt and Straw. Also, explore sand dunes containing artifacts from a Cecil B. DeMille film, and tour extreme memorabilia at The Museum of Death.

programme.title: Wild Travels
programme.desc: Travel the country and discover the wackiest oddities it has to offer.

programme.title: Grannies on Safari
programme.desc: For centuries Istanbul has stood as a confluence of Christian, Moslem, and Byzantine cultures. Framed by the Marmara, Black, and Mediterranean Seas, and located on the Bosphorus Strait, the city is home to Hagia Sofia, and is a historical repository of art, culture, and ancient architecture.

programme.title: Andiamo! Uptown
programme.desc: Follow Donna to Santa Barbara, California, the "American Rivera,"&#157; and learn how to surf. Also, stay in an award-winning resort, and enjoy a dinner at restaurant that only uses organic local ingredients.

programme.title: Andiamo! Uptown
programme.desc: Donna visits Ocho Rios, Jamaica and explores the history, culture, adventure, and an amazing family-focused resort. She also highlights a wonderful Jam-Italian fusion restaurant that is a MUST when visiting Ocho Rios.

programme.title: Discovering the World
programme.desc: The people of Honshu Island find their balance in the words, wisdom, and harmony of Zen. Tour Honshu and explore historically fascinating cities like Kyoto and Hiroshima. Traditions, such as festivals, are very carefully preserved, but these days technology has swept through the country.

programme.title: The Silk Road
programme.desc: Travel to Liqian, located at the narrowest point of the Hexi Corridor. Legend states that Liqian is where descendants of Crassus' lost legions ended up. Next, visit the sprawling industrial metropolis of Lanzhou on the Yellow River, and sample an essential ingredient of Chinese culture - noodles.

programme.title: The Silk Road
programme.desc: After arriving in Xi'an, you find yourself in the very spot where the caravans of the ancient Silk Road ended their arduous journey. Visit the tomb of Qin Shi Huangdi, the first emperor to rule over a unified China, and discover his terracotta army as it stands guard over him for all eternity.

programme.title: The Toque 12
programme.desc: To some, the day has not truly begun without a hardy breakfast, and Phil knows all the best spots in Malaysia for the early risers out there, including L'Orangerie, the Mandarin Oriental Kuala Lumpur, the Sticky Wicket, and Marini Cafe.

programme.title: The Toque 12
programme.desc: Located in a 97-year-old building, Pampas Steakhouse at Old Malaya adds history and cozy ambiance to its dining experience. After Ren introduces Phil to Pampas, Chef Sajal highlights the restaurant's specialty dishes.

programme.title: Grannies on Safari
programme.desc: Spend exciting evenings in two of Japan's greatest cities: Tokyo and Kyoto. Experience Japan's temples, restaurants, and architecture. Retreat to Kusatsu, a mountain village at the base of a volcano, soak in therapeutic sulfur springs, and enjoy the ultimate spa experience.

programme.title: Grannies on Safari
programme.desc: Begin on a pilgrimage via bicycle rickshaw to Mother Ganges. Tour a factory famous for its Varanasi silk. In Bangalore, new experiences abound for Pat and Regina. Explore glamorous shopping, and join in on a traditional Indian wedding bachelorette party straight out of a Merchant-Ivory film.

programme.title: Rudy Maxa's World
programme.desc: Andaman Coast towns such as Phuket are well known to backpackers and sun seekers. Join Rudy as he paddles through hidden entrances of small islands to find mangroves few people ever see. Also, visit the dramatic limestone cliffs and rock formations jutting out from translucent waters of Phang-nga Bay.

programme.title: Finding Your Roots

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow UK
programme.desc: Included in the day's finds are a hoard of 200 typewriters, a suffragette doll dressed in clothes cut from women protestors prison uniforms, a ring worn by the daughter of Charles I.

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow UK
programme.desc: Michael Aspel and the team head for Cardiff. Amongst the treasures they discover a painting bought at a bootsale, a rare Prince of Wales tea caddy, and a dusty clock brought down from the attic.

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow UK
programme.desc: Amongst the items featured are an African witch doctor's ceremonial stick, a school boys exercise book from 1745 and a Scandinavian drinking tankard.

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow UK
programme.desc: Amongst the finds are a letter from Florence Nightingale, the racing silk of a famous jockey, a collection of old wedding photographs, and an Art Deco diamond brooch found in wartime London.

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow UK
programme.desc: Amongst the finds are a machine gun from World War One, a papal portrait rescued from the bottom of a river and a set of rare maps recording the early settlement of America.

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow UK

programme.title: The Rifleman

programme.title: The Rifleman

programme.title: The Rifleman

programme.title: The Rifleman

programme.title: Wild West Chronicles
programme.desc: When eleven-year-old Herman Lehmann is torn away from his frontier home by an Apache raiding party, he must adapt in order to survive. He soon forgets the life he unwillingly left behind and is adopted by an Apache family. For close to a decade, he lives as a Native American, learning their language and customs. Eventually, he even joins the same type of raiding party that separated him from his family. Though the years pass by, Herman's mother never gives up hope that she will be reunited with

programme.title: Wild West Chronicles
programme.desc: When young Texas Ranger Ira Aten is having trouble hunting down a ruthless outlaw, he enlists the help of John Hughes, an aloof rancher with an uncanny talent for tracking bad guys. The two men form an unlikely and unexpected bond while traversing the dusty path toward justice. With Hughes' guidance, Aten finally catches up to the outlaw and ends his reign of terror. Along the way, Hughes decides to become a Ranger himself and transforms from loner to lawman. Hughes' adventure with Aten is just

programme.title: Into The Wild Frontier

programme.title: The Outlaw Josey Wales
programme.desc: Josey Wales watches helplessly as his wife and child are murdered by Union men led by Capt. Terrill. Seeking revenge, Wales joins the Confederate Army. He refuses to surrender when the war ends, but his fellow soldiers go to hand over their weapons -- and are massacred by Terrill. Wales guns down some of Terrill's men and flees to Texas, where he tries to make a new life for himself, but the bounty on his head endangers him and his new surrogate family.

programme.title: The Law and Jake Wade
programme.desc: Bandits Clint Hollister (Richard Widmark) and Jake Wade (Robert Taylor) botch a robbery that ends with Clint imprisoned. Despairing, Jake leaves the loot in an abandoned village and quits crime for good. Now working for the law, Jake helps free his old cohort from jail. Clint doesn't want the treasure to go to waste, however, so he forces Jake and his girlfriend, Peggy (Patricia Owens), to lead him to the stash. But to unearth the money, they'll have to get past dangerous Comanche warriors.

programme.title: The Saga of Hemp Brown
programme.desc: A U.S. cavalry officer, Hemp Brown (Rory Calhoun), runs into some serious trouble when the party of civilians and troops he's bringing to a nearby Army fort is ambushed. A woman is killed during the gunfight, and money is stolen by the bandits. The leader of the gang, Jed Givens (John Larch), is an acquaintance of Brown, who implicates the fugitive during the subsequent trial. But Brown is court-martialed and booted from the Army. Brown tracks down Givens to restore honor to his name.

programme.title: Angry Planet

programme.title: Angry Planet
programme.desc: Definitely our wildest episode so far. Here’s the deal: Mt. Elgon is an 14,000 foot volcano in eastern Kenya. We propose a safari/ascent of the dormant volcano. Here’s the first kicker: High on the the flanks of the volcano is Kitum Cave – a cave created by the most unique form of erosion – Elephant Erosion. For millennia elephants have been going into the cave to carve it out with their tusks in order to eat the salty rock. Here’s the second kicker: the cave is thought to be the home of the Ebola Marberg virus – the world’s most deadly virus. So here’s the plan: We are going enter the cave, at night, wearing full Bio-Hazard suits to protect us from the virus.

programme.title: Angry Planet

programme.title: Five Little Chicks - Super Simple and more
programme.desc: Five Little Chicks is great for nap time and for practicing counting down from five. Try singing along when it’s time for a nap.

programme.title: Rosey & The Rowdy Rustlers Tour Bus - Super Simple and more
programme.desc: Rosey and The Rowdy Rustlers are coming to town to kick off their concert tour! But while driving out on the open road, a giant stray tumbleweed gets in the path of their tour bus, making a mighty mess! Rosey, Roxie, and Rude Jenny will have to take a detour to Carl’s Car Wash! With a quick mess assessment, Carl’s suds-sational car wash is ready to give Rosey and The Rowdy Rustlers’ vehicle a good scrub-a-dub! The band leaves as happy customers, and just in time for the concert!

programme.title: Who Took The Cookie? (Finny the Shark) - Super Simple and more
programme.desc: Finny’s friends are playing a game of Who Took The Cookie From The Cookie Jar. Who do you think took the cookie? The seahorse, the starfish, the octopus, the walrus, the dolphin, or the crab? Let’s play and find out!

programme.title: Moolissa The Underwater Cow - Finny The Shark and more
programme.desc: Finny’s class finds out they have a new special friend coming for a visit, a cow named Moolisa! Being from land, it can be tricky for Moolisa to do everything that Finny’s friends do in the sea, but Moolisa will try her best to share in all the experiences of life in the ocean.

programme.title: Five Little Monsters Jumping On The Bed - Super Simple and more
programme.desc: Here’s a silly and slightly spooky version of the classic kids’ rhyme, Five Little Monkeys, with monsters instead of monkeys. It’s a great song for practicing counting down from five, and for teaching little monsters (and children!) not to jump on the bed.

programme.title: Red Light Green Light - Super Simple and more
programme.desc: Tobee learned all about traffic safety in school today! He learned how to walk in a crosswalk, to look both ways before crossing a street, and what the colors of a traffic light mean. That reminds Caitie of a song about stopping and going called “Red Light, Green Light.” Together, they sing and dance, stopping and starting again and again. Tobee and Caitie have a blast, and you can join the fun, too! Green light!

programme.title: Twinkle Twinkle Little Star - Super Simple and more
programme.desc: “Twinkle, twinkle little star. How I wonder what you are?” Lulu the Owl and Juno the Star become fast friends in this Super Simple version of one of the world’s most popular nursery rhymes.

programme.title: With My Heart - Super Simple and more
programme.desc: Feel the warmth and learn the five senses in this heart warming Super Simple sensory song for kids. It’s also a perfect song to celebrate all parents. I can hear with my ears. I can see with my eyes.

programme.title: Weepy French Onion Soup - Super Simple and more
programme.desc: Today, the Bumble Nums are going to make Weepy French Onion Soup. But the four weepy onions they need are VERY tricky to find. Will Humble, Grumble, and Stumble be able to spot all the onions and get back to the kitchen without crying too much? Watch and see!

programme.title: Seven Steps - Super Simple and more
programme.desc: Counting to seven has never been more memorable and fun! Learn numbers with this simple counting song.

programme.title: Put On Your Shoes - Super Simple and more
programme.desc: Here’s a Super Simple Song all about getting dressed! Sing along as we put on our shoes, jacket, hat, and scarf and then head out for a walk with the family.

programme.title: With My Heart - Super Simple and more
programme.desc: Feel the warmth and learn the five senses in this heart warming Super Simple sensory song for kids. It’s also a perfect song to celebrate all parents. I can hear with my ears. I can see with my eyes.

programme.title: Let's Count To 100 - Super Simple and more
programme.desc: We are learning to count to 100 with Finny the Shark! This is a perfect numbers song for young kids and preschoolers! Let’s count to 100. Counting’s so much fun. Let’s count to 100. We’ll start from number 1.

programme.title: Let's Count To 100 - Super Simple and more
programme.desc: We are learning to count to 100 with Finny the Shark! This is a perfect numbers song for young kids and preschoolers! Let’s count to 100. Counting’s so much fun. Let’s count to 100. We’ll start from number 1.

programme.title: Super Wings
programme.desc: World Aircraft is flying through the Himalayas when an order comes in from a house near Mt Everest. Jett and Dizzy are sent to deliver some mega-sized art supplies to two brothers who want to make a mega-sized "Good Luck" card for their Dad. He's about to climb Mt. Everest! But when World Aircraft gets into trouble and crashes, it's going to take more than a Supercharge to save it!

programme.title: Super Wings
programme.desc: A girl in Bogota asks for a spray-painting drone. When her jealous little dog steals the controls causing the drone to paint the town, they must get it back before he makes a huge mess. Jett and Donnie travel to Germany to bring a long wig to a girl named Kirsten. She wants to dress up as the famous princess, Rapunzel. Kirsten learns that it takes more than a wig to be a princess.

programme.title: Super Wings
programme.desc: A girl in Switzerland orders an early release copy of a new book by a teenage adventure author. Discovering that the last chapter is missing, the Super Wings help her find the author. A boy named Pavel whose hobby is repairing watches, orders a special Super Wings wristwatch. Pavel takes them to see the Astronomy Clock in the Old Town Square, but they discover the old clock has stopped.

programme.title: Super Wings
programme.desc: When a boy and his grandmother board a luxury train in South Africa with self-inflating balloons, some animals cause the balloons to inflate, sending the train up into the sky. A girl named Kate from Bunbury, Australia, asks for a scanner that can go underwater. Jett and Mira learn that Kate needs it to analyze her favorite dolphin, Ninotchka. But Ninotchaka takes off after an undersea earthquake.

programme.title: Super Wings
programme.desc: On Halloween, a boy in California orders his own fully-functional Super Wings costume. But a haunted house scares Donnie haywire and he gets a giant pumpkin decoration stuck on his head! Jett and Paul fly to Cairo, where a girl has ordered a special item to help her lead a tour of the Cairo Museum for her friends. But the girl's little brother accidentally brings the mummies and sphinx to life!

programme.title: Super Wings
programme.desc: Two boys who are pals that live in Greece, are huge mythology fans. They ask for a card game with mythological creatures that are holograms. When the creatures get too big, they threaten to destroy a town. A boy who loves everything about wizards wants to be a real wizard with a real magic wand. The closest Storm can come to making one is a toy wand with some Supercharge energy stored in it.

programme.title: Super Wings
programme.desc: A girl in Moscow with a rock collection orders a special box with compartments labeled for the coolest rocks ever. When she discovers that her collection doesn't have the last three rocks needed to complete the box, the Superwings must help her get them by going on a trip into space, because the rocks needed are space rocks!

programme.title: Super Wings
programme.desc: A Dad in Brazil orders a special explorer's outfit for his son. A huge wave rushes up the Amazon River and threatens the river animals and a village. A girl living in Dezhou China, is excited that she and her parents are hosting the Chinese New Year celebrations for the whole family. But traffic jams on land, sea, and air cause trouble for all the family members from other provinces.

programme.title: Super Wings
programme.desc: A girl, Akaja, asks the Super Wings for molds that can make multi-colored ice blocks. She wants the blocks to build an igloo that holds a playground that holds a playground. A boy named Nuno in Porto, Portugal orders a large, clear, unbreakable bottle. He wants to build a model boat in a bottle. When the SWs and he shrink down to build the boat, the bottle is accidentally knocked out of a window.

programme.title: Super Wings
programme.desc: A girl in Kuala Lumpur orders a special swing for the new baby orangutan to play on. But a mishap makes the orangutan become king-sized and it escapes from the zoo. A girl in Mexico named Daniela orders some cat treats shaped like marigolds. Jett and Astra learn that Daniela wants to use the treats to attract the attention of her recently deceased cat, Sanchez, for 'Day of The Dead.'

programme.title: Super Wings
programme.desc: A girl named Ruma in Bangladesh orders a Table Top Cricket Game with moving players. Ruma and her friends like the game, but want to play a real game of cricket. A boy from France orders a machine that can make nutritious cotton candy out of vegetables. It's for a world culture fair being held at the boy's school. The boy puts too many vegetables into the machine creating a catastrophe!

programme.title: Super Wings
programme.desc: World Aircraft returns to World Airport for a special day of races in front of the Super Wings' biggest fans - the children of the world! The contestants are in for a test when a surprise challenger shows up. A girl living in Michoacán, Mexico orders a pair of costume butterfly wings and a flower crown. She hopes the butterflies will think she's a butterfly too and will land on her flower crown.

programme.title: Super Wings
programme.desc: Jett delivers a Pasta Making Machine to a boy in Pisa, Italy. The machine is to help him win a contest for the biggest noodle in town. Jett delivers a Spinning Top to a boy, Kenta, in Sapporo, Japan! Kenta wants to use the top to enter the Spinning Top Competition at the local Snow Festival. Things go well until Golden Boy enters the competition, by making himself into a spinning top.

programme.title: Super Wings
programme.desc: Jett and Jet Pet join a girl and her father to look for a lost tiger kitten's mother in the jungle of Madhya Pradesh, India - the same jungle from the girl's favorite book, "The Jungle Book" by Rudyard Kipling. Jett delivers drone wings to a girl, Lea, in Geneva. They are for her car, Leo, who now can fly, a skill that he hopes will win him the "Best Car of the Year" award at a local Motor show.

programme.title: Super Wings
programme.desc: Jett and Jett Pet deliver a fan letter to Trevor, a young musician and old friend of the Super Wings. When he learns his biggest fan is in the hospital, Trevor really wants to go perform for him too, but their tour bus breaks down in the middle of the desert. A welcome ceremony at the National Aviation Museum in Seoul is being held for Korea's oldest plane. But the old plane crashes on the way!

programme.title: Super Wings
programme.desc: Jett and Jett Pet are called to Stockholm, Sweden, to deliver a special kid-friendly smartphone so a girl named Lily can keep in touch with her friend, Karin, during a snowstorm. It's a special night when the super moon appears in the sky. The Super Wings have plans to make it very special for all the kids on Earth by making a giant Super Wings sign on it.

programme.title: Super Wings
programme.desc: It's a special night when the super moon appears in the sky. The Super Wings have plans to make it very special for all the kids on Earth by making a giant Super Wings sign on it. When Golden Boy tries to outdo Jett on a delivery, he ends up moving everyone in Whittier, Alaska to Hawaii! Jett, Paul, and the pets work hard to get the people, their building, and a dog back to where they belong.

programme.title: Baby Einstein Classics
programme.desc: At 12 months, babies can identify specific sounds, and they re also starting to match the sounds they hear with the objects that make them. Meet the Orchestra -- First Instruments presents your little "maestro" with a playful introduction to the sights and sounds of a real orchestra -- from the clashing of cymbals to the purring of the cello and everything in between!

programme.title: Baby Einstein Classics
programme.desc: As each day passes, babies' awareness of the world around them -- and all the wonders it holds -- continues to develop. Wild animals are especially intriguing and World Animals captures the spirtit of little ones' fascination with wild creatures as it introduces them to animals living in the jungle, in the ocean, and on the savannah.

programme.title: Baby Einstein Classics
programme.desc: Take your little explorer on an exotic musical expedition to meet wild animals from across the globe. Journey to the jungle, savannah, desert and forest.

programme.title: Baby Einstein Classics
programme.desc: Set off on a fun-filled "field trip" with your baby to discover animals found at the park, on the farm, and in your own backyard.

programme.title: Baby Einstein Ocean Explorers
programme.desc: Cal, Neptune, Opus, Dean, Tinker and others learn about the flora and fauna of the ocean through STEAM-based principles and real-life footage.

programme.title: Baby Einstein Classics
programme.desc: Share a rainbow of sights and sounds with your baby as you discover the wonders of color together! Join puppet host "Vincent Van Goat" as he creates six Van Gogh masterpieces exploring red, green, blue, yellow, orange, and purple. World Of Colors is a spirited celebration of creative expression.

programme.title: Baby Einstein Classics
programme.desc: As little ones grow, their innate sense of wonder grows too, creating an occasion for celebration with each new discovery. Baby Galileo Discovering the Sky introduces them to that remarkable canopy above us: the sun and moon, fluffy clouds, shimmering stars, colorful planets and whirling galaxies.

programme.title: Baby Einstein Classics
programme.desc: One of the greatest joys you'll ever experience is bonding with your baby during those calm, quiet moments. Lullaby Time: Soothing Sounds for Baby provides comforting sights and sounds as you relax and share this precious downtime.

programme.title: Baby Einstein Classics
programme.desc: As their communication skills continue to develop, little ones begin to recognize common words and may point to objects or pictures when these items are named. Baby Wordsworth playfully taps into your little one's natural curiosity and introduces 30 words from around the home -- both spoken and in sign language. This engaging program presents little ones with familiar objects in the kitchen, playroom, yard and more -- from the living room window to their favorite teddy bear.

programme.title: Baby Einstein Classics
programme.desc: Within the first year of babies' lives, their physical development begins to kick into high gear as they explore ways to physically interact with the world around them. BABY'S FIRST MOVES is a joyful celebration of their movement milestones -- including first steps, clapping, jumping, dancing and more.

programme.title: Baby Einstein Classics
programme.desc: As little ones' curiosity about the world blossoms, you can help sow the seeds of discovery with Baby MacDonald: A Day on the Farm. Featuring live-action images, nursery rhymes and fun-filled puppet shows, this playful, interactive program introduces little ones to the exciting sights and sounds of a real working farm.

programme.title: Baby Einstein Classics
programme.desc: As their communication skills continue to develop, little ones begin to recognize common words and may point to objects or pictures when these items are named. Baby Wordsworth playfully taps into your little one's natural curiosity and introduces 30 words from around the home -- both spoken and in sign language. This engaging program presents little ones with familiar objects in the kitchen, playroom, yard and more -- from the living room window to their favorite teddy bear.

programme.title: Baby Einstein Classics
programme.desc: As babies turn one year old, they continue to develop their communication skills. Meanwhile, with you as their guide, they also begin to explore the world beyond their front door. Baby's Favorite Places presents them with a playful introduction to 20 words for the names of familiar locations from the market, to the library, to the local park.

programme.title: Five Little Ducks Nursery Rhymes & Kids Songs - Boom Buddies
programme.desc: Five Little Duck, Bingo The Dog, Incy Wincy Spider and many more Boom Buddies rhymes! Happy singing!

programme.title: Nursery Rhymes for Children + More Kids Song - Jelly Bears
programme.desc: Join Jelly Bears as they dance to preschool rhymes like Five Little Teddy Bears, Johny Johny and more!

programme.title: Super Supremes Old Macdonald & More Kids Songs
programme.desc: Kids! Are you ready to go round the Farmees town? Hop on-board this Red Wheels On The Bus to enjoy your fun learning ride.

programme.title: Booya Non-Stop
programme.desc: Follow the hilarious adventures of five adorable kooky monsters who live under a kid's bed. Living under the bed is easy and kind of safe but these adorable characters are impulsive, curious, and always ready for fun adventures. With a playful dog who is bent on messing up their plans, the group keeps venturing out, finding ways to dodge the snoopy sniffy, creating chaos and laughter around them.

programme.title: Super Supremes Wheels On The Bus & More Popular Kids Song
programme.desc: Do you kids know Wheels on the Bus? Learn this classic nursery rhyme with The Supremes, plus many other fun songs and dances.

programme.title: Mr. Fruit Nursery Rhymes & Kids Learning Videos
programme.desc: Meet Mr. Fruit, the fun cartoon character! Watch nursery rhymes, learning videos, and baby songs with him. Mr. Fruit makes learning a joyous adventure for kids by providing a perfect blend of fun and early development. It's exciting and helpful for toddlers to learn while having fun. Enjoy the playful way of growing and learning!

programme.title: Mr. Shape Educational Rhymes and Baby Songs
programme.desc: Mr. Shape is a hub of beloved nursery rhymes and engaging content designed to educate preschoolers through the magic of animated cartoons! Children can sing along to popular rhymes and baby songs such as 'Five Little Babies', 'This is the Way', and many more! Our fun videos are made to help kids learn while having a good time. We also teach them important family values and how to stay healthy.

programme.title: Little Red Car - Unlimited Songs
programme.desc: Each this animated children's rhymes compilation with colorful characters, along with catchy music.

programme.title: Sonic Underground
programme.desc: A speed enhancing fluid deemed Sonic Tonic is created by Robotnik to make him as fast as Sonic so that he may finally catch him.

programme.title: Sonic Underground
programme.desc: The Hedgehogs congregate on the Floating Island in their latest effort to find their mother. But Sleet and Dingo also have an interest in this island.

programme.title: Sonic Underground
programme.desc: Sonic becomes aggravated that maybe their efforts of fighting for freedom seem pointless. Sonia suggests that they go to the Oracle for advice.

programme.title: Sonic Underground
programme.desc: Robotnik has his eyes set upon Queen Aleena's crown jewel, but Sonic, Manic and Sonia plan to retrieve the gem before Robotnik.

programme.title: Sonic Underground
programme.desc: Sonic, Manic and Sonia find an abandoned baby in an alley and begin to look for the baby's parents. Manic becomes very fond of the child.

programme.title: Sonic the Hedgehog
programme.desc: When Robotnik launches a spy-orb that will uncover the Freedom Fighter's Knothole location, Sonic and Robot launch themselves up to the orbiting spy-sphere aboard a home-made rocket powered by Robotnik's discarded booster engine.

programme.title: Sonic the Hedgehog
programme.desc: When Sally uncovers Robotnik's plan to seed the cloud with toxic rain, they attempt a daring mission to sabotage the dirigible-like craft being transported by land vehicles. Mission fails when Sally, Bunnie and Antoine are captured.

programme.title: Sonic the Hedgehog
programme.desc: Robotnik severely upsets the ecosystem above and below the surface, when he sends explosive, Robotnik snake probes drilling deep under ground in search of oil lakes.

programme.title: Sonic the Hedgehog
programme.desc: Tails brings a new friend home to Knothole - a baby Terra-Pod who has strayed from the herd that is currently migrating. Sonic, Sally, and some of the other Freedom Fighters return the Baby-T to its mom and the rest of the herd.

programme.title: Sonic the Hedgehog
programme.desc: Sonic reluctantly takes Tails with him to the Big City to find some cotter pins for the Freedom Fighters catapults. While in the City, Sonic is nearly eaten alive by his old dog Muttski, now a roboti-sized guard dog in the Junk Heaps

programme.title: Sonic the Hedgehog
programme.desc: The Freedom Fighters, with new addition, Dulcy, develop a De-Roboti-sizer and try it out on Bunnie. It's a success and, against Sally's wishes, Sonic battles Robotnik's newest weapon, a Virtual Reality Shriek-Bot.

programme.title: Sonic the Hedgehog
programme.desc: Sonic attempts a solo rescue to prevent prisoners from being roboti-sized and is hit by a Memory Scrambler that erases short-term memory.

programme.title: Sonic the Hedgehog
programme.desc: After a disastrous failed mission, Sonic laments that they can't go back and prevent Robotnik's take-over. Dulcy tells them they can and takes Sonic and Sally to the Floating Island where they find the "Time Stones".

programme.title: Sonic Underground
programme.desc: A speed enhancing fluid deemed Sonic Tonic is created by Robotnik to make him as fast as Sonic so that he may finally catch him.

programme.title: Sonic Underground
programme.desc: Sonia discovers that the watch she received from her friend is actually a monitoring device for the Robotnik Intelligence Agency.

programme.title: Rainbow Ruby
programme.desc: There is a new menu at the restaurant, which is very popular. There is a long line in front of the restaurant but there weren't enough staff members to serve the villagers the food. Rainbow Ruby turns into a waitress using her magic suitcase and serve tables kindly. // Rainbow Village is full of marbles. The marbles that Rainbow Ruby spilled in her room poured down into the village from up above the sky! Rainbow Ruby uses her magic suitcase to transform into a paramedic.

programme.title: Rainbow Ruby
programme.desc: Ling Ling is worried about the play planned for tonight. There are no costumes ready for the villagers so it's hard to figure out which roles they're playing. Rainbow Ruby transforms into a costume designer and designs costumes for her friends. // It is very windy in Rainbow village and Paige, a paper doll, is flying to wherever the wind takes her. She is so light that she is blown away by the wind. Rainbow Ruby uses her magic suitcase to become a fire fighter and rescue Paige.

programme.title: Rainbow Ruby
programme.desc: Ling Ling is worried that the villagers are all in low spirits. Ruby thinks of fun games to play but the villagers say that they have already played all of the games too many times. Rainbow Ruby transforms into a singer. // Ling Ling is worried because of the "Secret Present Swapping Party" that Thunderbell is preparing. Thunderbell forgot to send out the invitations. Rainbow Ruby turns into a mail carrier and delivers the invitations.

programme.title: Rainbow Ruby
programme.desc: Ling Ling tells Rainbow Ruby that a Rainbow Ball Tournament is about to start soon but he is worried because the field is covered with pokey pods. Then Ellie tells her she lost her needles, Gina her hat, and Thunderbell his wheels. Who could have taken away all these things?

programme.title: Rainbow Ruby
programme.desc: Ling Ling is waiting for Rainbow Ruby, looking worried. It turns out that Mr. Sloth's best friend Chirpee is depressed. Chirpee wants to fly high but is struggling due to his small wings and lack of means to learn how to fly. // Ling Ling tells Rainbow Ruby that the dress that the paper doll Paige was planning to wear to Kiki's dance party is torn. Paige very much wants to go to the party but she cannot go without a dress.

programme.title: Rainbow Ruby
programme.desc: When Ruby and Choco arrive at the village, they find a very worried looking Ling Ling. Gina, Ellie, and Thunderbell went camping in Rainbow Forest but they saw a monster in the forest, got scared and had to run away. // Ruby is struggling to make a doll with her dad. Ruby and Choco get their suitcase and as Ruby swings her umbrella, there they are, in front of Rainbow fountain. Mayor Ling Ling tells her that today is the day of Princess Kiki's ballet performance.

programme.title: Rainbow Ruby
programme.desc: Ruby is looking at shoes that are now too small for her but she is unwilling to throw away. Ling Ling is worried about Thunderbell. His scooter broke so he is not able to carry out his work as a mail carrier. // Gina calls Rainbow Ruby because today is the day to show their appreciation to Mayor Ling Ling. It took a long time for the village friends to prepare an amazing painting for the mayor. Unfortunately, Thunderbell accidentally destroyed the painting while delivering it.

programme.title: Rainbow Ruby
programme.desc: Gina, Thunderbell, and Choco try to cheer Jessy up but their efforts are of no use. While talking to Mayor Ling Ling, Rainbow Ruby realizes that today is the day that Jessy first arrived in Rainbow Village! Jessy was upset that her friends forgot such a meaningful day to her. // Ling Ling has caught a bad cold and was sneezing all over the place. Rainbow Ruby uses her magic suitcase and transforms into a doctor and cares for him.

programme.desc: Ling Ling is worried about the play planned for tonight. There are no costumes ready for the villagers so it's hard to figure out which roles they're playing.
